market.note: Inf economy, AH flipping, IO set pricing; JS-rendered topic list

wiki.access_note: Both accessible via curl with browser User-Agent (HTTP 200); WebFetch returns 403. hcwiki.cityofheroes.dev serves a redirect page — homecoming.wiki is the live canonical host.

lore.cosmology_summary: CoH's mythological timeline begins with the goddess Merulina arriving from space; early human gods split into factions — the benevolent ones defeated the Banished Pantheon (who fed on suffering rather than worship). A Second War of Gods saw Tielekku teach magic, spawning the Oranbegan civilization (now Circle of Thorns enemy group). The nation of Mu practiced eugenics for magical power. Historical threads run from Egyptian/Roman antiquity through the founding of Arachnos, Circle of Thorns, and modern signature characters.

story_bible.note: Internal Paragon Studios reference document (~534 pages). Never officially released. A tabletop RPG writer with partial access published excerpts after the 2012 shutdown; hosted on the wiki as the best public window into official lore.

signature_heroes.role: Trainers, task force contacts, and occasional allies throughout Paragon City

major_factions.name: Banished Pantheon
major_factions.origin: First War of Gods; gods who fed on suffering

major_factions.name: Circle of Thorns
major_factions.origin: Oranbegan civilization; exiles who fled underground

major_factions.name: Arachnos
major_factions.origin: Modern shadow organization; Lord Recluse's faction

major_factions.name: Mu Mystics
major_factions.origin: Exiled nation who practiced eugenic breeding for magic

character_customization.summary: CoH's customization is cosmetic-first: mechanics are fixed per archetype/power, but appearance is deeply layered across four independent systems.

systems.name: Costumes
systems.details: Core visual system. Created at character creation; revised at tailor NPCs (Icon/Facemaker/Rita Mayfair). Minor color changes free; body sliders cost more. Costume Change Tokens earned every 10 levels. Up to 10 costume slots (base 1, unlocked at levels 20/30/40, +1 via Halloween event). Switch instantly with /cc #.

systems.name: Power Customization
systems.details: Recolor particle effects of powers — fire can be blue/purple, energy can be yellow. Per-power-set palettes (bright, dark, ice-themed, earth-themed). Applied per-archetype since same power set can look different by class.

systems.name: Weapon Customization
systems.details: Added Issue 11. Choose weapon model (including NPC faction variants) for weapon-based sets: broadsword, katana, claws, dual pistols, assault rifle, and more. No mechanical change, purely aesthetic.

systems.name: Animation Customization
systems.details: Change visual animation style of certain attacks independently of effects. Layers onto power and weapon customization for full aesthetic control.

character_customization.mod_relevance: This customization depth explains why CoH mod categories skew heavily toward visual/texture mods — the game's architecture was designed from the start for deep cosmetic self-expression. Costume mods, texture replacements, and UI skin mods map directly onto these four layers.
